injuries: Yeremy Pino (Spain) — shoulder injury sustained in group stage vs Uruguay, ruled out for remainder of tournament
Medina (Argentina) — calf problem since last-32 win vs Cape Verde, has not featured since, doubtful/race against time for the final
suspensions: None for either squad — FIFA reset yellow-card counts after the quarterfinals for the expanded 2026 format; only a semifinal red card would have ruled a player out, and neither team had one
lineupHints: Spain predicted XI: Simón; Porro, Cubarsí, Laporte, Cucurella; Rodri, M. Ruiz; Yamal, Olmo, Baena; Oyarzabal
Argentina predicted XI: E. Martínez; Montiel, Romero, Lisandro Martínez, Tagliafico; Paredes; De Paul, Fernández, Mac Allister; Álvarez, Messi
Lamine Yamal and Pedro Porro trained apart from the main Spain squad in New Jersey as workload management after finishing the semifinal heavily fatigued, but both are expected to start
motivationFlags: Argentina chasing back-to-back World Cup titles — would be only the third team ever to repeat as champions, after Brazil (1958/62) and Italy (1934/38)
Lionel Messi, 39, would become the oldest outfield player to start a World Cup final if he starts as planned
Spain chasing a first World Cup title since 2010
travelFlags: Neutral-venue final at MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, NJ — no home-ground advantage for either side
Both sides on 4 days' rest since their July 15 semifinals (Spain beat France 2-0; Argentina beat England 2-1 with a late comeback)
